report:
**EQUITY RESEARCH MEMORANDUM**
**TO:** Investment Committee
**FROM:** Senior Equity Research Analyst
**DATE:** February 22, 2026
**TICKER:** AMZN (Amazon.com, Inc.)
**SUBJECT:** Institutional Investment Thesis & Factor Valuation

---

### **1. PERFORMANCE & RISK BENCHMARKING**
*   **Total Returns vs. SPY:**
    *   **1-Year:** +14.2% (AMZN) vs. +11.5% (SPY).
    *   **3-Year (CAGR):** +18.4% (AMZN) vs. +9.2% (SPY).
    *   **5-Year (CAGR):** +4.8% (AMZN) vs. +10.1% (SPY). *Note: Underperformance driven by the 2022–2023 tech reset and current "Capex Winter."* [Source: 1.11, 1.25, Yahoo Finance]
*   **Risk Metrics:** 
    *   **Beta (5Y Monthly):** 1.14 (Moderate systematic risk premium). [Source: MarketBeat]
    *   **Sharpe Ratio (TTM):** 1.18 (Adjusted for Feb 2026 volatility). [Source: Financhill]
*   **Price Z-Scores (as of Feb 20, 2026 Close: $210.11):**
    *   **50-Day MA Z-Score:** **-0.95** (Price is nearly 1σ below short-term mean).
    *   **200-Day MA Z-Score:** **-0.36** (Long-term structure remains intact but weakening). 
    *   *Status: No extreme technical exhaustion (Z < -2.0) detected.* [Source: Phase 3 Calculations]

### **2. MULTI-FACTOR PROFILE**
*   **Momentum:** **Weak/Negative.** AMZN is currently underperforming 82% of the Consumer Discretionary sector over a 3-month lookback. Price is 6.2% below the 200-day SMA ($223.97). [Source: Phase 3.1]
*   **Quality:** **Top-Tier.** ROIC of **14.23%** consistently exceeds WACC of **11.34%** (Spread: +2.89%). Leverage is exceptionally conservative with a **0.16 D/E ratio** and a **35.2x interest coverage ratio**. [Source: 1.13, 1.19]
*   **Value:** **Deep Value (Historical Context).** TTM P/E of **29.3x** is in the **bottom 15th percentile** of its 5-year range (Avg: 58.4x). EV/EBITDA of **15.6x** is a ~27% discount to its 5-year average. [Source: 1.1, 1.33]

### **3. SENTIMENT & SIGNAL QUANTIFICATION**
*   **NLP Sentiment Score:** **+0.25 (Cautiously Bullish)**
    *   *Positive Catalyst:* AWS re-acceleration (+24%) and Ad-revenue growth (+22%).
    *   *Negative Catalyst:* "Sticker shock" from the **$200B 2026 Capex guidance** (Source: CEO Andy Jassy, Q4 Call).
*   **Analyst Consensus:** 
    *   **Mean PT:** $272.50 (+29.7% upside).
    *   **High/Low Range:** $325 (Argus) / $175 (DA Davidson).
    *   **Recent Activity:** 5 "Buy" reiterations vs. 2 "Target Lowers" post-FY25 results. [Source: Phase 2.3]

### **4. TECHNICAL REGIME & INTRADAY VOLATILITY**
*   **Market Regime:** **Mean-Reverting.** ADX at **22.44** indicates the absence of a strong trend; the asset is currently range-bound between $196 and $221. [Source: Investing.com]
*   **Support/Resistance (Volume Profile):**
    *   **S1 (Standard Deviation):** $201.15.
    *   **S2 (30-Day Floor):** $196.00.
    *   **R1 (Intraday Node):** $211.17.
    *   **R3 (Mean Resistance):** $221.39 (Confluence of 20-day SMA). [Source: Phase 3.4]
*   **Volatility Projection:** ATR of **$6.80** suggests an expected intraday range of **$203.31 – $216.91**. [Source: Barchart]

### **5. TAIL RISK & CATALYST MODELING**
*   **Historical Max Drawdown (MDD):** -51.9% (2022). Current drawdown from 52-week high: -18.2%.
*   **Options Signal:** Bullish bias (Put/Call **0.64**). Significant **$831.9K premium** call sweep at the $200 strike (Exp: Mar 6) suggests a tactical floor. [Source: Cheddar Flow]
*   **Upcoming Catalysts:**
    *   **Alexa+ Subscription Launch:** Estimated $2B+ high-margin ARR potential.
    *   **Supreme Court Tariff Ruling (Feb 20):** Overturning of cross-border tariffs acts as a specific tailwind for Q1 2026 international retail margins. [Source: MarketBeat]

### **6. SUGGESTED ENTRY POINT PRICING**

| Tier | Strategy | Entry Price | Distance | R:R Ratio | Condition Status |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **TIER 1** | **Aggressive** | **$204.50** | -2.67% | 2.5:1 | **NEAR** (RSI 39.3, <3% to S1) |
| **TIER 2** | **Base Case** | **$196.00** | -6.71% | 4.2:1 | **NOT YET** (Requires macro drift) |
| **TIER 3** | **Conviction**| **$188.50** | -10.28% | 6.1:1 | **NOT YET** (Requires RSI < 35) |

*   **Tier 1 Basis:** Derived from the **$201.15 S1 support node** and 1-day standard deviation boundary.
*   **Tier 2 Basis:** Represents a **15% margin of safety** from DCF fair value ($231) and alignment with the major 30-day volume floor ($196).
*   **Tier 3 Basis:** Alignment of **P/E at ~25x** (historical floor), weekly RSI oversold (<35), and psychological support near the 2025 breakout level.

### **7. SYSTEMATIC VERDICT**
**FINAL RATING: ACCUMULATE**

**Justification:** Amazon is currently a "Quality-Value" play masked by "Growth" headwinds. While the market has penalized the stock for its aggressive **$200B AI infrastructure spend**, our analysis shows that **ROIC (14.23%)** remains comfortably above **WACC (11.34%)**, confirming that the capital deployment is value-accretive, not dilutive. The current **29.3x P/E** represents an attractive entry for a business growing EPS at a **22.6% 5-year CAGR**. We favor an **Accumulate** rating, utilizing the **Tier 1 ($204.50)** level to build initial exposure, as technicals show the stock is mean-reverting from extreme oversold conditions (RSI recovered from 6.7 to 39.3 in 72 hours). The primary risk remains the FCF suppression through 2026, but the expansion in AWS margins (35%) and advertising (22% growth) provides a robust fundamental floor.
